title: ceanothus integerrimus var. macrothyrsus
intro: Ceanothus integerrimus var. macrothyrsus, also known as Mountain White Cedar, is a shrub-like plant native to North America, belonging to the Rhamnaceae family and being a variety of the Ceanothus integerrimus species. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which are known to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Mountain White Cedar are attributed to its ability to boost the immune system and act as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating various health issues such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant's herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, and it can also be dried and used in potpourri or as a herbal smoke.
